intro:

Here's a paragraph about Crataegus spp. (Hawthorn):

Crataegus spp. (Hawthorn) is a flowering plant in the family Rosaceae, commonly used in herbal medicine. The health benefits of Hawthorn include the potential to lower blood pressure, improve heart function, and reduce symptoms of anxiety and insomnia. The bioactive constituents of Hawthorn include flavonoids (such as quercetin and vitexin), triterpenoids, and oligomeric proanthocyanidins (OPCs), which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Hawthorn include teas, tinctures, capsules, and tablets, often used to support cardiovascular health.

The exact classification of Oxyacantha obtusata may vary depending on the source, but it is generally accepted to be a member of the Rosaceae family. Further classification may include its order (Rosales), class (Magnoliopsida), and kingdom (Plantae).
